stem: A factory produces automotive parts using two sources. Robot Arm Station 1 handles 79% of production with a known defect rate of 1.7%. Robot Arm Station 2 handles 21% of production, but its defect rate is uncertain — recent quality audits suggest it lies between 7.6% and 11.8%, depending on raw material batch quality. A randomly selected automotive part is found to be defective. What is the probability it came from Robot Arm Station 2?

ground_truth.answer: P(Robot Arm Station 2 | defective) ranges from 0.543 to 0.649 as Robot Arm Station 2's defect rate varies from 7.6% to 11.8%. Point estimate at 9.7%: 0.603.

solution_steps: Apply Bayes' theorem: P(B|def) = P(def|B)×P(B) / [P(def|A)×P(A) + P(def|B)×P(B)].
The interval [0.543, 0.649] captures the range of posterior probabilities.
common_failure_modes: Using only the midpoint defect rate and providing a single answer
Comparing defect rates directly without applying Bayes' theorem
